project_name: Inventory APK — Newland MT90 Industrial Inventory Manager

frameworks: Android Jetpack (Room, WorkManager, Navigation, Lifecycle, Hilt)
key_dependencies: Newland Scanner Broadcast API (nlscan.action.*) — Hardware scanner integration

target_audience.primary_persona: Warehouse operators and logistics floor workers in Ukrainian industrial facilities (factories, distribution centers, retail warehouses) using Newland MT90 handheld terminals — gloved operation, noisy environments, intermittent or no network connectivity
secondary_personas: Warehouse managers and supervisors needing inventory oversight, audit results review, and sync status monitoring across locations
IT administrators responsible for deploying and managing fleets of 50-200 handheld devices via Ndevor MDM
Ukrainian SMB business owners seeking affordable post-1C inventory management replacement
Newland AIDC Ukrainian importers and channel partners requiring white-label B2B solutions for their warehouse clients
pain_points: Prohibitive total cost of ownership for enterprise warehouse mobility (Zebra/Honeywell: $1,500-2,800/device + $3-5/device/month MDM — a 50-device fleet exceeds $150K in year one)
Universally terrible mobile UX on industrial handhelds — SAP EWM requires pinch-zoom (3+ companies exist just to fix it), Honeywell SwiftDecoder fails scans, Zebra DataWedge causes software conflicts
No true offline-first operation — most solutions are cloud-first and fail in cold storage, underground, or RF-interference zones
Scanner software conflicts and complex configuration (DataWedge exclusive hardware control, SwiftDecoder scan failures, CT60 camera-vs-scanner confusion)
Post-1C migration urgency in Ukraine — banned Russian software since 2017 with no viable local mobile-first warehouse management alternative
Complex device fleet deployment requiring IT specialists and expensive MDM subscriptions that don't scale for SMBs
SaaS pricing instability (Sortly: documented 10x price increases, 257% hikes over 2 years) — unpredictable costs destroying budget planning
Steep training requirements for legacy warehouse systems (SAP EWM transaction codes) causing high onboarding cost and seasonal worker turnover
goals: Scan barcodes and update inventory in real-time with sub-2-second scan-to-confirmation
Work reliably without internet connectivity — zero data loss during offline periods
Receive goods, conduct physical inventory audits, and transfer stock between locations using simple scan-driven workflows
Deploy and manage entire device fleets within minutes via zero-touch QR provisioning
Export inventory data to CSV/Excel for reporting and integration with existing business systems
Achieve sub-1-hour onboarding for new floor workers with intuitive Ukrainian-language interface
Replace banned 1C warehouse module with modern, affordable, mobile-first alternative
target_audience.usage_context: Industrial warehouse floor operations in Ukrainian SMB facilities — used during receiving shifts, stock audits, and transfers. Operators hold Newland MT90 handheld terminals in one hand while wearing gloves, scanning barcodes on pallets, shelves, and incoming shipments in noisy environments with intermittent or no Wi-Fi/cellular connectivity. Sessions range from 15-minute spot checks to 8-hour full inventory audits. Devices are fleet-managed via Ndevor MDM with QR-code zero-touch provisioning.

product_vision.one_liner: An affordable, offline-first Android inventory management app purpose-built for Newland MT90 industrial handheld terminals, targeting Ukrainian warehouse workers migrating from banned 1C software
product_vision.problem_statement: Ukrainian SMB warehouses need mobile inventory management but face an impossible choice: enterprise solutions (Zebra/Honeywell/SAP) are prohibitively expensive at $150K+ for a 50-device fleet, consumer apps (Sortly) lack industrial capabilities and have unpredictable SaaS pricing, and the previously dominant 1C software is banned in Ukraine since 2017. No affordable mobile-first solution exists that combines rugged hardware scanner integration, true offline-first operation, zero-touch fleet provisioning, and Ukrainian language support.
product_vision.value_proposition: Vertically-integrated warehouse digitization bundle — Newland MT90 hardware + purpose-built inventory app + free Ndevor MDM — delivering 50%+ TCO reduction vs enterprise alternatives with industrial-grade UX (scan-driven auto-advance, 60-80dp gloved touch targets, multimodal feedback) and true offline-first ACID architecture that guarantees zero data loss in any connectivity environment. Works standalone without ERP dependency and scales to multiple B2B clients via white-label Product Flavors from a single codebase.
success_metrics: Sub-2-second scan-to-confirmation latency for barcode operations on MT90 hardware
Zero data loss during offline operation and sync recovery (outbox pattern + idempotency keys)
Sub-1-hour onboarding time for new warehouse floor workers
5-minute device provisioning from unboxing to operational via Ndevor MDM QR enrollment
50%+ TCO reduction vs Zebra/Honeywell enterprise solutions for 50-device fleet deployments
Successful deployment across 5+ Ukrainian SMB warehouse clients within first year
Support for 10,000+ inventory items per location with smooth UI performance
95%+ uptime for sync operations with exponential backoff and jitter resilience

existing_features: Newland MT90 hardware scanner integration (NewlandScannerManager singleton with BroadcastReceiver, F6 hardware button handler, 300ms debounce, SharedFlow<ScanResult>, programmatic trigger)
Room database with 5 entities: InventoryItem, Category, Location, InventoryOperation, OutboxEntry — with complete DAO layer
Repository pattern (InventoryRepository interface + InventoryRepositoryImpl)
Offline-first outbox pattern for guaranteed sync delivery with ACID compliance and idempotency keys
Multi-provider sync engine: SyncEngine + SyncWorker (WorkManager) + SyncProviderFactory + SyncSettings/SyncSettingsManager
4 sync providers implemented: HTTP API (Retrofit), FTP (commons-net), WebDAV, Local Folder
5 sync providers stubbed for Phase 3: OneDrive, Google Drive, Email/SMTP, Telegram Bot, 1C HTTP
3 sync serializers: CSV, Excel (FastExcel), JSON
Core scan-to-record workflow (ScanScreen + ScanViewModel + ScanUiState)
Goods receiving operation (ReceivingScreen + ReceivingViewModel + ReceivingUiState)
Physical inventory count/audit operation (AuditScreen + AuditViewModel + AuditUiState)
Industrial Design System (IndustrialTheme, IndustrialColors, IndustrialTypography with 60-80dp touch targets)
Multimodal scan feedback (sound + vibration + visual via ScanFeedbackManager)
Manual theme switching (System/Light/Dark via ThemePreferenceManager)
Reusable UI components (IndustrialButton, ScanResultCard, ScanStatusBar)
Sync settings UI (SyncSettingsScreen + ProviderSettingsScreen + SyncSettingsViewModel)
Network layer (Retrofit InventoryApiService, AuthInterceptor, AuthTokenProvider, IdempotencyInterceptor, NetworkMonitor, ApiResult sealed class)
Hilt DI modules (DatabaseModule, NetworkModule, ScannerModule, SyncModule)
GitHub Actions CI pipeline (ci.yml)
Architecture documentation (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
known_gaps: No automated tests (zero unit tests, zero UI tests, zero integration tests — test dependencies declared but no test files exist)
No item management CRUD UI (add/edit/delete inventory items screen)
No search and filter functionality for inventory items
No category management UI
No stock transfer between locations operation screen
No low-stock alert system or notifications
No reporting and analytics dashboard
No multi-location/warehouse management UI
No supplier management
No stock movement history / audit trail viewer UI
No data export UI (serializers exist but no user-facing export screen)
No multi-user access or role-based permissions
No cloud sync / multi-device synchronization beyond sync engine
No Ukrainian language localization (strings appear to be in mixed Ukrainian/English without formal i18n)
No white-label Product Flavors architecture (planned as key differentiator, build.gradle has placeholder comment only)
No Ndevor MDM integration or zero-touch provisioning setup documented
OneDrive, Google Drive, Email/SMTP, Telegram Bot, and 1C HTTP sync providers are stubs only (TODO Phase 3)
No complete navigation graph connecting all screens
No batch scanning mode
No error handling/retry UI for failed sync operations
No app icon or splash screen (using defaults)
technical_debt: Duplicate InventoryApplication.kt files in two packages (com.inventory.app.InventoryApplication AND com.inventory.InventoryApplication)
Zero test coverage across 63 Kotlin source files and 5,315 lines of code — high regression risk
KAPT Java 17 workaround in build.gradle.kts with extensive --add-opens flags for 10+ JDK modules (fragile, breaks on JDK updates)
Kotlin 1.9.22 and Compose BOM 2023.10.01 are outdated — newer stable versions available (Kotlin 2.x, Compose BOM 2024+)
No database migration strategy — schema version 1 with no migration path for future changes
Stub sync providers throw NotImplementedError at runtime — needs graceful handling or feature flags to prevent user-facing crashes
BuildConfig API_BASE_URL points to placeholder example.com domain
build.log file committed to repository root — should be gitignored
Mixed Ukrainian/English code comments and UI strings without consistent i18n/l10n framework
No .editorconfig or code formatting standards enforced
Should consider KAPT → KSP migration for faster annotation processing and better Kotlin 2.x compatibility
No ProGuard/R8 rules for third-party libraries (fastexcel, commons-net) beyond Android defaults

alternatives: Zebra Technologies (DataWedge + TC5x/MC Series) — market leader, ~$2,820/device + $3-5/device/month MDM, DataWedge scanner conflicts and vendor lock-in
Honeywell Mobility Edge (CT45/CT60/CK65 Series) — premium tier, SwiftDecoder scan failures, $1,500-2,800/device, hardware reliability issues
SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) Mobile — enterprise-only, terrible mobile UX requiring pinch-zoom, $500K+ implementation, no offline mode
1C:Enterprise Warehouse Module — banned in Ukraine since 2017, desktop-first, no mobile capability, cybersecurity risk
Sortly (Generic Consumer Inventory Apps) — consumer-grade, no industrial scanner support, extreme SaaS price hikes (documented 10x increases), unreliable sync
RFgen Mobile WMS — requires existing ERP (SAP/Oracle/Dynamics), ~$4,500 starting price, not standalone, no Eastern European focus
Torgsoft (Ukrainian Trade Automation) — local Ukrainian alternative, desktop-first, no industrial scanner support, no offline mobile capability
differentiators: 50%+ TCO reduction through vertically-integrated Newland MT90 + custom app + free Ndevor MDM bundle — eliminates both hardware premium and MDM subscription costs
Industrial UX excellence: scan-driven auto-advance, 60-80dp gloved touch targets, multimodal feedback (sound+vibration+visual) — purpose-built for one-handed warehouse operation
True offline-first ACID architecture with outbox pattern and idempotency keys — designed from ground up for disconnected warehouse environments, not bolted on as afterthought
Zero-touch QR provisioning via free Ndevor MDM (5 min unboxing to operational) — no expensive MDM subscription required
Ukrainian-market-first positioning with local language, local support, competitive UAH pricing, and direct post-1C migration path
Standalone operation without ERP prerequisite — works immediately with local Room database, integrates progressively via multi-provider sync engine
White-label Product Flavors for scalable B2B delivery — single codebase producing distinct branded APKs per warehouse client (planned)
competitive_context.market_position: First-mover as the only mobile-first Ukrainian warehouse scanning solution targeting the massive post-1C migration market. Positioned in the underserved gap between prohibitively expensive enterprise solutions (Zebra/Honeywell/SAP at $1,500-2,800/device + MDM subscriptions) and inadequate consumer apps (Sortly). Leverages exclusive partnership with Newland AIDC Ukrainian importer for vertically-integrated hardware + software + MDM bundle at 50%+ cost reduction. Eastern European e-commerce growth (15%+ annually) driving warehouse automation demand in historically underserved markets creates favorable timing.
competitor_pain_points: Prohibitive total cost of ownership for enterprise warehouse mobility — $1,500-2,800/device + $3-5/device/month MDM subscriptions + annual support contracts. A 50-device fleet exceeds $150K in year one.

Universally terrible mobile UX on industrial handhelds — SAP EWM requires pinch-zoom (3+ companies exist just to fix it), Honeywell SwiftDecoder fails to scan, Zebra DataWedge causes software conflicts

Unreliable offline-to-online sync and data loss in warehouse connectivity dead zones — most solutions are cloud-first with offline as afterthought, not designed for cold storage or underground facilities
Scanner software conflicts and configuration complexity — DataWedge exclusive hardware control locks out other apps, SwiftDecoder fails to scan 'any barcode', CT60 activates camera instead of scanner
Complex device deployment requiring IT specialists and expensive MDM — manual provisioning 'extremely time-consuming' and 'does not scale' for growing device fleets
SaaS pricing instability exemplified by Sortly — documented 10x price increases, 257% hikes over 2 years, account cancellation difficulties, charges without notification
Post-1C vacuum in Ukraine — banned Russian software leaving no viable local mobile-first warehouse management alternative for mass-migrating businesses
ERP dependency blocking SMB adoption — RFgen and SAP EWM require existing enterprise backend that most Ukrainian SMBs do not have

technical: Must run on Android 7.0+ (API 24 minSdk) to support full range of Newland MT90 device variants
Newland-specific broadcast intent API (nlscan.action.*) for scanner integration — not portable to other scanner brands without abstraction layer
KAPT annotation processing required for Hilt/Room — slower builds, Java 17 module access workarounds needed
Offline-first architecture requires careful conflict resolution strategy for concurrent edits across devices during sync
Must operate reliably in environments with intermittent connectivity, cold storage, underground areas, and RF interference from metal shelving
Industrial UI must support 60-80dp touch targets for gloved operation on 5-inch MT90 display
Room database migrations needed for every schema change — no versioning/migration strategy in place yet
Scanner integration cannot be tested in emulator — requires physical Newland MT90 device for end-to-end testing
No Google Play Store distribution — deployed via Ndevor MDM sideloading or direct APK install
resources: Solo developer project (single contributor: Artem Filimets, 64 commits)
Heavily AI-assisted development (auto-claude automated build pipeline for feature implementation)
No dedicated QA, UX designer, or testing resource — zero test coverage currently
Hardware testing requires physical Newland MT90 devices — limited by device availability
Ukrainian market focus limits initial addressable market but reduces competition significantly
dependencies: Newland AIDC hardware partnership for MT90 device supply, SDK, and firmware support
Newland broadcast intent API (nlscan.action.SCANNER_RESULT) stability across firmware versions
Ndevor MDM platform for zero-touch fleet provisioning and OTA app updates (free, bundled with Newland devices)
Backend API server for multi-device sync (currently placeholder URL: api.inventory.example.com — needs to be built or integrated per client)
FTP/WebDAV servers at client sites for alternative sync provider support
Android SDK and build toolchain (AGP 8.13.2, Kotlin 1.9.22, JDK 17)
Client-specific branding assets and API endpoints for each white-label Product Flavor
